stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated technologies include transformers and safetensors, indicating modern ML infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the ongoing advancements in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Anthropic has completely cut off access to its models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
The action was taken following a government order citing national security concerns.
The restriction applies to all foreign nations, including blocking access for Anthropic employees.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has decommissioned its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration.
The Commerce Department expressed concern that a Fable 5 'jailbreak' posed a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability has been discovered in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The vulnerability is described as being among the most severe ('about as critical as they come').

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian forces.
The incident highlights the rapid integration of AI modules onto military drones and robots.
This development signals a significant, real-world shift toward autonomous systems in modern warfare.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The research introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed for autonomous scientific discovery.
It posits that the key bottleneck for advanced scientific automation is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text-generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's local device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the strategy and future of its next-generation console, Project Helix.
Microsoft is considering a major restructuring, including the possibility of spinning off the Xbox brand into a separate entity.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces Agents-K1, a new pipeline designed for scientific knowledge orchestration in LLM agents.
Addresses a gap in current research where LLM agents oversimplify scientific papers (e.g., only using abstracts or citation counts).
Aims to convert raw documents into 'agent-native' knowledge by capturing complex elements like entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived by composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal systems theory (Operad theory).

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion across model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent (with tools/planning) rather than just a model call.
This concept is being practically implemented in production coding agents, such as those in Anthropic's dynamic workflows.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these AI agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models offline on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: My first 24 hours with Siri AI on the Mac
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: The article reviews the initial experience with the new Siri AI integration on macOS 27 Golden Gate.
The author expresses skepticism regarding previous iterations of Siri AI, but notes a potential shift with the new features.
The review is early-stage, as the author is still in the process of testing the new AI capabilities.

stories.title: Apple’s new AI photo editing tools mostly work, for better and worse
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: Apple has introduced its first serious AI photo editing features in iOS 27.
The reviewer suggests the new AI tools are relatively modest compared to competitors like Google's Pixel phones.
The article implies that the current state of AI photo editing technology is not yet fully mature or ready for widespread adoption.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX's ownership structure has shifted, implying a move toward public market expectations.
The focus of investor interest is heavily tied to SpaceX's potential in Artificial Intelligence.
The article prompts discussion on the future trajectory of the company given its new financial pressures.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant investment in data center projects ($130 billion) is facing public opposition.
The protests highlight growing community pushback against the expansion of AI infrastructure.
The narrative suggests that successful local opposition provides a sense of political influence.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) where lexical/semantic similarity fails for complex reasoning.
It introduces Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a novel post-training framework.
RA-RFT aims to enable language models to learn reasoning by analogy, moving beyond simple knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics, specifically articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping policies for physically complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It focuses on how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The work investigates the relationship between interface design and an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility.
This addresses the current resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale manual process in social and behavioral sciences.
The study tested LLM-generated analyses against original data from 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective not fully captured by the other models.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing AI's influence on human cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly expanding coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It proposes that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just restricting actions during execution.
Key techniques mentioned include specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools in robotics.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly expanding coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The paper investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
It introduces 'Recovery Subspace Dimensionality' ($k_t$) as a novel metric to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using a sequentially trained ResNet-18 on Split CIFAR-100, analyzing representational drift and recovery across multiple tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Presents a novel framework for aerial wildfire suppression planning.
Combines a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model to predict spatially varying fire spread.
Integrates a gradient-based optimization module to design targeted aerial intervention strategies.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continually adapt their knowledge and behavior to progressive environmental changes.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
Proposes Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a new post-training framework.
Argues that lexical/semantic similarity is insufficient for complex reasoning, as different problems may share underlying patterns.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how individual training samples influence a model's output, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs.
The technique distills gradient influence rankings from decoders into the encoder structure.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents, where atomic tool calls clutter the reasoning trace.
Introduces 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface designed to abstract low-level dataflow from the LLM.
Aims to improve context efficiency and reduce the burden on the model by moving beyond traditional step-wise tool invocation tracing.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Proposes that an LLM's direct answer to a complex query should match the answer derived from a stated decomposition of that same query.
Offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal mathematical structures (Operad theory).

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for a low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: US bans differential privacy in Census data
stories.author: nl
bullet_points: The US government has banned the use of differential privacy in Census data.
This decision represents a significant shift in how sensitive demographic data is protected and released.
The move impacts the application and adoption of privacy-preserving technologies in large-scale government data collection.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions made by AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The tool functions by analyzing the commit history of a codebase to infer AI involvement.
The article is a self-promotion for the tool, linking to both the source code and a discussion thread.
